subtopic: Ramsar Convention and New Sites in India
title: Mechanisms under Ramsar Convention
focus: Ramsar List criteria, Montreux Record status in India, and Wetland City Accreditation details.
rationale: This section forms a distinct conceptual block detailing the structural mechanisms of the Ramsar Convention, including the Montreux Record and WCA, which are highly testable static-current linkages.
sectionExcerpt: Details the Ramsar List, noting the UK has the most sites, Brazil's Rio Negro is the largest, and India has 96 (highest in Asia). The Montreux Record lists wetlands facing ecological change due to human interference; in India, it includes Keoladeo National Park and Loktak Lake, while Chilika Lake was removed. Wetland City Accreditation (WCA) is a voluntary system approved at COP12 (2015) that is valid for 6 years.
evidenceBullets: India has 96 Ramsar sites, the highest number in Asia.
The Montreux Record currently includes India's Keoladeo National Park and Loktak Lake; Chilika Lake was removed after successful conservation.
Wetland City Accreditation (WCA) was approved at COP12 in 2015 and remains valid for 6 years before requiring renewal.

subtopic: Ramsar Convention and New Sites in India
title: Outcomes of Ramsar COP15 and India's Contributions
focus: Key declarations, strategic plans, and India's specific resolutions and achievements at Ramsar COP15.
rationale: Groups the international outcomes of COP15 with India's specific participation, resolutions, and achievements (like WCA for Indore and Udaipur), forming a coherent international relations and environment block.
sectionExcerpt: Highlights COP15 outcomes including the Victoria Falls Declaration, 5th Strategic Plan 2025-2030, and the Indo-Burma Ramsar Regional Initiative (IBRRI) supported by IUCN's BRIDGE project. India introduced a resolution on sustainable lifestyles aligned with Mission LiFE, was elected to the Standing Committee (2025-2028), and saw Indore (Sirpur Lake) and Udaipur awarded Wetland City Accreditation.
evidenceBullets: The Victoria Falls Declaration acknowledges the ecological, social, and economic roles of wetlands.
IBRRI Member States include Cambodia, Lao PDR, Myanmar, Thailand, and Vietnam, with the IUCN serving as the Secretariat.
India successfully introduced a resolution promoting sustainable lifestyles for the wise use of wetlands, in line with Mission LiFE.
Indore and Udaipur were awarded Wetland City Accreditation; Udaipur is surrounded by five major wetlands including Pichola and Fateh Sagar.

subtopic: Ramsar Convention and New Sites in India
title: New Ramsar Sites in India
focus: Newly designated Ramsar sites in India, their geographical locations, and specific ecological or historical details.
rationale: A purely geographical and ecological block focusing on the newly added Ramsar sites. The specific flora, fauna, and geographical markers make this highly testable for match-the-following questions.
sectionExcerpt: Lists new Ramsar sites including Khichan, Siliserh, and Menar in Rajasthan, along with sites in Bihar and Chhattisgarh. Khichan Wetland hosts wintering demoiselle cranes. Menar Wetland Complex is a freshwater monsoon wetland hosting critically endangered vultures. Siliserh Lake is a human-made wetland in the Sariska tiger reserve buffer zone, created in 1845 on a tributary of River Ruparel.
evidenceBullets: Khichan Wetland in Rajasthan's Thar Desert hosts wintering flocks of migratory demoiselle cranes.
Menar Wetland Complex in Udaipur, Rajasthan, hosts critically endangered white-rumped and long-billed vultures.
Siliserh Lake in Rajasthan is a human-made wetland located in the buffer region of the Sariska tiger reserve.
Other newly listed sites include Udaipur Lake, Gogabeel Lake, and Gokul Reservoir in Bihar, and Kopra Jalashay in Chhattisgarh.
